title: NVIDIA NGC personal keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: NVIDIA NGC API key detected in variable assignment. NGC API keys provide access to NVIDIA GPU Cloud services including container registries, pre-trained AI models, and GPU compute resources. Exposing these keys allows unauthorized access to expensive GPU resources, proprietary models, and your NGC account. Store credentials securely using environment variables or secret management systems.

NVIDIA NGC API key detected in docker login command. Docker login commands to nvcr.io (NVIDIA Container Registry) expose NGC API keys used for authentication. These keys grant access to GPU-optimized containers and private repositories. Exposing them allows unauthorized downloads of proprietary containers and potential abuse of your NGC account. Use docker credential helpers or environment variables.

NVIDIA NGC API key detected in NGC CLI configuration command. NGC CLI config set commands expose API keys for NVIDIA GPU Cloud authentication. These keys provide access to GPU compute resources, model registry, and container registry. Exposing them enables unauthorized usage of expensive GPU resources and access to proprietary AI models. Use secure credential storage.

NVIDIA NGC bearer token detected in authorization header. Bearer tokens for NGC API provide authenticated access to NVIDIA GPU Cloud services. Exposing these tokens allows unauthorized API access, GPU resource consumption, and potential data exposure. Tokens should be obtained dynamically and never hardcoded.

Generic long authentication token detected in NVIDIA NGC context. Long alphanumeric tokens (40-80 characters) in NGC-related code often represent API keys or authentication credentials for NVIDIA GPU Cloud. Exposing these allows unauthorized access to GPU compute resources, container registries, and AI model repositories. Verify token type and rotate immediately.
